examples.title: Dynamic 1D Array Allocation
examples.description: Demonstrates basic dynamic memory allocation for a 1D array in C++
examples.explanation: This program dynamically allocates an integer array of size entered by user at runtime. Memory is allocated on heap using 'new', used, then freed using 'delete[]' to prevent memory leak.

examples.title: Dynamic 2D Array (Row Pointer Method)
examples.description: Allocates a 2D array using array of pointers
examples.explanation: Creates a 2D array using pointer-to-pointer technique. Each row is independently allocated, allowing for flexible row sizes. Memory must be freed in reverse order - first rows, then the pointer array.

examples.title: Class with Dynamic Memory (Rule of Three)
examples.description: Implements a String class demonstrating proper memory management
examples.explanation: This String class demonstrates the Rule of Three. The destructor frees memory, copy constructor performs deep copy, and assignment operator checks self-assignment before copying. This prevents memory leaks and dangling pointers.

examples.title: Smart Pointer Example
examples.description: Demonstrates automatic memory management using unique_ptr
examples.explanation: Smart pointers provide automatic memory management. unique_ptr has exclusive ownership - automatically deletes when out of scope. shared_ptr uses reference counting and only deletes when all references are gone. This prevents memory leaks.
